items.prompt: What did God call the light in Genesis 1?
items.explanation: God called the light Day and the darkness Night in Genesis 1.

items.prompt: What were humans told to rule over in Genesis 1?

options: Fish, birds, livestock, and the earth's creatures
Only the birds of the sky
Only the trees of the garden

correct_answer.value: Fish, birds, livestock, and the earth's creatures

items.explanation: Genesis 1 says humanity was given dominion over animals on the earth, in the sea, and in the sky.

items.prompt: Where did God place the man after forming him?
items.explanation: Genesis 2 says God planted a garden in Eden and placed the man there.

items.prompt: Which tree did God command the man not to eat from?

options: The tree of the knowledge of good and evil

correct_answer.value: The tree of the knowledge of good and evil

items.explanation: The command in Genesis 2 specifically forbids eating from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

items.prompt: After the man and woman ate the fruit, what did God place east of the garden of Eden?
items.explanation: Genesis 3 says cherubim and a flaming sword guarded the way to the tree of life.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 1.

content.text: God gathered the waters together so that dry ____ appeared.

items.explanation: Genesis 1 says the waters were gathered so dry land could appear.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 2.

content.text: God formed the man from the dust of the ____.

items.explanation: Genesis 2 describes God forming the man from the dust of the ground.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 2.

content.text: The woman was made from one of the man's ____.

items.explanation: Genesis 2 says God made the woman from a rib taken from the man.
items.teaching_note: This question focuses on the direct narrative detail in Genesis 2.

items.prompt: Complete the sentence from Genesis 3.

content.text: The serpent spoke to the ____ in the garden.

items.explanation: Genesis 3 begins with the serpent speaking to the woman.

items.prompt: Which image best represents God separating light from darkness?

options.label: A bright horizon dividing day from night
options.alt: Symbolic scene showing light separated from darkness

options.label: A wooden boat on rough water
A stone city gate at sunset
options.alt: Ancient stone gate with buildings beyond it

options.label: A shepherd leading sheep across hills
options.alt: Shepherd walking with sheep in open country

correct_answer.value: A bright horizon dividing day from night

items.explanation: Genesis 1 describes God creating light and separating it from darkness.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Which image best represents the serpent speaking in the garden?

options.label: A serpent coiled near a fruit tree in a garden
options.alt: Symbolic serpent near a fruit tree in a garden setting

options.label: A lion resting beside a riverbank
options.alt: Lion lying beside a calm river

options.label: A farmer gathering grain in a field
options.alt: Farmer harvesting grain in a field

options.label: A stone altar with fire on top
options.alt: Stone altar with flames burning on it

correct_answer.value: A serpent coiled near a fruit tree in a garden

items.explanation: Genesis 3 places the serpent in the garden speaking to the woman about the forbidden tree.
items.image_accuracy_note: Images are symbolic teaching aids and are not exact historical reconstructions.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 1 events in the order they appear.

items: Lights are set in the sky

value: Lights are set in the sky

items.explanation: These events follow the day-by-day order of Genesis 1.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 2 events in the order they appear.

items: God places the man in the garden
God brings animals to the man
God forms the man from dust
God plants a garden in Eden

value: God forms the man from dust
God plants a garden in Eden
God places the man in the garden
God brings animals to the man

items.explanation: Genesis 2 first describes the man being formed, then the garden, then the man's placement there, and later the animals being brought to him.

items.prompt: Put these Genesis 3 events in the order they appear.

items: The woman gives fruit to the man

value: The woman gives fruit to the man

items.explanation: Genesis 3 moves from the serpent's question, to eating, to opened eyes, and then to hiding from God.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 1 term with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: What God called the gathered waters
What God called the dry land

value.right: What God called the gathered waters
What God called the dry land

items.explanation: Genesis 1 names the light, darkness, dry land, and gathered waters.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 2 item with the correct description.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: River listed as one of the four flowing from the garden
Garden region where God placed the man
River associated with the land of Havilah

value.right: Garden region where God placed the man
River associated with the land of Havilah
River listed as one of the four flowing from the garden

items.explanation: Genesis 2 names Eden and several rivers connected to the garden account.
items.teaching_note: Only details stated in Genesis 2 are used here.

items.prompt: Match each Genesis 3 figure with the correct outcome.

content.instruction: Match each item with the correct description.
right_items: Sent out from the garden of Eden
Told he would return to the ground
Told of multiplied pain and desire for her husband
Cursed to go on its belly and eat dust

value.right: Cursed to go on its belly and eat dust
Told of multiplied pain and desire for her husband
Told he would return to the ground
Sent out from the garden of Eden

items.explanation: Genesis 3 records distinct consequences for the serpent, the woman, the man, and the pair together.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Genesis 1.

content.statement: Plants and fruit trees appear before the sun, moon, and stars are mentioned.

items.explanation: Vegetation appears on the third day, while the lights in the sky are described on the fourth day.

items.prompt: Evaluate this statement from Genesis 3.

content.statement: After eating the fruit, the man and woman sewed fig leaves together for coverings.

items.explanation: Genesis 3 says their eyes were opened, and they made coverings from fig leaves.

items.prompt: Find the factual mistake in this Genesis 3 statement.

content.statement: When they heard God in the garden, the man and woman immediately walked out to meet him.
content.instruction: Spot the error in this statement.
options: They hid among the trees instead of walking out to meet him.
They were not in the garden when God came.
Only the serpent heard God in the garden.
God did not speak to them after they ate.

correct_answer.value: They hid among the trees instead of walking out to meet him.

items.explanation: Genesis 3 says they hid themselves among the trees of the garden when they heard God.
items.teaching_note: This tests the shame-and-hiding detail after disobedience.
